title: Two-barrier stress test design is correct and should be defended
description: relay/tests/stress.rs uses start_barrier (all N clients must receive DirectoryList before any send) and done_barrier (all sends complete before any drain). This design makes the received == sent*(N-1) assertion exact with no fudge factor. A contributor might suggest removing start_barrier to 'simplify' the test, but without it early senders reach fewer than N-1 peers and the assertion becomes non-deterministically wrong. Why this choice is correct: the two-barrier protocol is the minimal structure that makes the count exact. The alternative (probabilistic assertion with tolerance) would hide real routing bugs behind noise.
close_reason: decision already recorded in CLAUDE.md / architecture is intentional; no code change needed
